title: The most useful feature
author: Greenice33
body_snippet:
Can I import and run multiple connections at once? Example
54.202.139.61 Administrator ?Uuvfy?Pj=K 
54.212.215.27 Administrator-aP*LQSa&J 
54.2012.11.171 administrator pbox-Ycjh%M 
If not, make this a very useful feature for everyone

iterations.description: The issue combines multiple old enhancement requests without a current-version repro or clear expected behavior, so it is not actionable yet.
The issue combines multiple legacy feature requests without clear, current-version reproducible steps or a single scoped expected behavior.
Feature request to allow bulk import and immediate connection from text/list (e.g. pasted IPs), which is more efficient than CSV for quick tasks.

comments.author: Greenice33
comments.snippet:

comments.author: Greenice33
comments.snippet: And help how to make it so that when you create a new connection his name had been edited immediately . And not later through the context menu

notes:
AI triage (codex): The issue combines multiple old enhancement requests without a current-version repro or clear expected behavior, so it is not actionable yet.
Approach: Request retest on v1.79.x and split into two reproducible asks: bulk connection creation/launch workflow and immediate inline rename when adding a new connection.
AI triage (codex): The issue combines multiple legacy feature requests without clear, current-version reproducible steps or a single scoped expected behavior.
Approach: Request retest on v1.79.x with exact steps and expected vs actual results, then split into separate issues for bulk connection creation/launch and immediate inline rename on new connection.
AI triage (gemini): Feature request to allow bulk import and immediate connection from text/list (e.g. pasted IPs), which is more efficient than CSV for quick tasks.
Approach: Implement a 'Quick Import' dialog that accepts multiline text, attempts to parse connection details (IP, User, Pass), and allows adding them to the tree or connecting immediately.
